The best trees are the following five : Arjuna, Ajakarna,
Priyaka (Priyafigu), Dhava (Lythrum fruticosum) and Udumbara
(Glomerous fig-tree). One of these or any other of excellent quali-
ties that has grown on white (or red) or black soil should be select-
ed. A Brahmana should approach alone in the first part of the

348 Bfhai SamhitU
night such a tree, worship it according to the rules and recite the
following hymn, touching the tree.
SFft? 9ft ^TTf5T &T9: ^rftcT 9: I
I
e^sns? 5%?i srfcr^rm^
"Hail to all beings living in this tree t Salutation to you.
May you change your abode after receiving this gift offered by me.
O great tree, hail to thee ! The king elects thee for (making) the
banner of the Lord of Gods. Kindly accept this worship."
[There is an under-current of deep humanism in this hymn.
